Title: Detect probable duplicate patients via deterministic match on identifier plus name
Preconditions: Duplicate-detection job configured; two records share national identifier with name variants
Test_Steps: 1. Load source dataset containing record A and record B sharing the same national identifier | 2. Run the deterministic duplicate-detection pass keyed on identifier+last name | 3. Open the candidate-duplicate worklist | 4. Inspect the matched pair and match key
Test_Data: A=Maria Tester (NID 900112233); B=Maria Testor (NID 900112233)
Expected_Result: The pair A/B is flagged as a probable duplicate with match reason "identifier+name" and surfaced on the duplicate worklist for steward review

Title: Probabilistic fuzzy match scores near-duplicate names above threshold
Preconditions: Probabilistic matching enabled with configurable score threshold of 0.85
Test_Steps: 1. Load two records with similar demographics but no shared identifier | 2. Run the probabilistic matcher using name, DOB and gender | 3. Review the computed match score | 4. Confirm placement on the worklist relative to threshold
Test_Data: A=Jon Smith DOB 1980-01-02 M; B=John Smyth DOB 1980-01-02 M; Threshold=0.85
Expected_Result: The pair scores at or above 0.85 and is flagged as a probable duplicate; the score and contributing fields are displayed

Title: Boundary: match score exactly at threshold is treated as a match
Preconditions: Probabilistic matcher threshold configured at 0.85 inclusive
Test_Steps: 1. Construct a record pair engineered to yield score exactly 0.850 | 2. Run the matcher | 3. Inspect classification of the pair
Test_Data: Computed score=0.850; Threshold=0.85 (inclusive)
Expected_Result: Pair at score 0.850 is classified as a match (threshold is inclusive); pair at 0.849 would be non-match

Title: Negative: distinct twins with same DOB and surname are not auto-merged
Preconditions: Two genuine sibling/twin records exist with shared surname and DOB but distinct identifiers
Test_Steps: 1. Load twin records sharing surname and DOB but differing first name and identifier | 2. Run duplicate detection | 3. Review classification | 4. Attempt any auto-merge action
Test_Data: A=Anna Tester NID 700100 DOB 2015-05-01; B=Anita Tester NID 700101 DOB 2015-05-01
Expected_Result: Records are NOT auto-merged; at most flagged for manual review with differing identifier noted; no silent data loss occurs

Title: Decision table for merge eligibility across identifier and demographic agreement
Preconditions: Merge rule engine configured with identifier-match and demographic-match inputs
Test_Steps: 1. Apply rule combinations: identifier match Y/N x DOB match Y/N x name match Y/N | 2. For each combination run the eligibility evaluation | 3. Record the engine decision (auto-merge / review / no-action)
Test_Data: Combos: (ID=Y,DOB=Y,Name=Y)->auto; (ID=Y,DOB=N,Name=Y)->review; (ID=N,DOB=Y,Name=Y)->review; (ID=N,DOB=N,Name=N)->no-action
Expected_Result: Each combination yields the documented decision; no combination with identifier mismatch results in auto-merge

Title: Survivorship rule keeps most-recent non-null value during dedupe consolidation
Preconditions: Survivorship policy: prefer most-recent non-null per field
Test_Steps: 1. Identify a confirmed duplicate pair where each holds different non-null phone numbers | 2. Run consolidation with survivorship policy | 3. Inspect the surviving golden record
Test_Data: A phone=555-0001 updated 2024-01; B phone=555-0002 updated 2025-06
Expected_Result: Golden record retains the most-recent non-null phone 555-0002; superseded value preserved in history/audit

Title: Required-field completeness check flags rows missing mandatory demographics
Preconditions: Completeness ruleset defines mandatory fields: identifier, name, gender, birthdate
Test_Steps: 1. Load a batch where some rows lack gender or birthdate | 2. Run the completeness validation pass | 3. Open the completeness report | 4. Inspect flagged rows and reason codes
Test_Data: Row 5 missing gender; Row 9 missing birthdate
Expected_Result: Rows 5 and 9 are flagged incomplete with field-level reason codes; completeness percentage is reported per field and overall

Title: Boundary: completeness threshold gate blocks load below configured percentage
Preconditions: Load gate configured to require >= 95% completeness on mandatory fields
Test_Steps: 1. Prepare a batch computed to yield 94.9% mandatory completeness | 2. Submit batch to the gated loader | 3. Observe gate decision | 4. Repeat with a batch at exactly 95.0%
Test_Data: Batch1 completeness=94.9%; Batch2 completeness=95.0%; Gate>=95%
Expected_Result: Batch1 is rejected by the gate; Batch2 at exactly 95.0% passes the gate (inclusive boundary)

Title: Validity check rejects birthdate in the future
Preconditions: Validity ruleset enforces birthdate <= load date
Test_Steps: 1. Load a record with birthdate set after today | 2. Run validity validation | 3. Inspect error report
Test_Data: Birthdate=2099-01-01; Load date=2026-07-01
Expected_Result: Record is flagged invalid with reason "birthdate in future"; record is quarantined and not promoted to target

Title: Boundary: age 0 (birthdate equals load date) is accepted as valid
Preconditions: Validity rule accepts birthdate up to and including load date
Test_Steps: 1. Load a newborn record with birthdate equal to load date | 2. Run validity validation | 3. Inspect result
Test_Data: Birthdate=2026-07-01; Load date=2026-07-01
Expected_Result: Record is accepted as valid (age 0 newborn); no future-date error is raised

Title: Gender code outside allowed value set is rejected
Preconditions: Validity rule constrains administrative gender to value set male|female|other|unknown
Test_Steps: 1. Load a record with gender code outside the value set | 2. Run validity validation | 3. Inspect reason code
Test_Data: Gender=X9; Allowed=male|female|other|unknown
Expected_Result: Record flagged invalid with "gender not in value set"; the offending code is captured in the error report

Title: Pairwise validation across format, range and code-set rules on demographics
Preconditions: Validity engine applies independent rules for format, range and code-set
Test_Steps: 1. Generate pairwise combinations of {phone format valid/invalid} x {postal range valid/invalid} x {country code valid/invalid} | 2. Load each combination | 3. Capture which rule(s) fire per row
Test_Data: Combos covering each pair of conditions at least once
Expected_Result: Each rule fires independently and correctly for its triggering condition; combined invalid rows accumulate multiple reason codes without masking

Title: Cross-field consistency: deceased flag true requires death date present
Preconditions: Consistency rule: dead=true implies deathDate not null and >= birthDate
Test_Steps: 1. Load a record with dead=true but null death date | 2. Run consistency validation | 3. Load a second record with dead=true and death date before birthdate | 4. Inspect both results
Test_Data: R1 dead=true deathDate=null; R2 dead=true birthDate=1990-01-01 deathDate=1980-01-01
Expected_Result: R1 flagged "death date required when deceased"; R2 flagged "death date precedes birth date"; both quarantined

Title: Referential consistency: encounter visit type matches an existing visit
Preconditions: Target schema enforces encounter.visit_id FK to visit table
Test_Steps: 1. Load encounters whose visit_id references a non-existent visit | 2. Run referential consistency check | 3. Inspect orphan report
Test_Data: Encounter E100 visit_id=V999 (no such visit)
Expected_Result: Encounter E100 reported as orphaned FK; row is rejected or routed to remediation; no orphan inserted into target

Title: Unit-of-measure consistency for vitals across source systems
Preconditions: Transformation maps source weight units to canonical kg
Test_Steps: 1. Load source rows with weight in lb and weight in kg | 2. Apply unit normalization transform | 3. Verify canonical stored values and UCUM unit code
Test_Data: Source: 154 lb and 70 kg
Expected_Result: 154 lb is converted to 69.85 kg (within tolerance) and 70 kg passes through; both stored with UCUM code kg; no double conversion

Title: Source-to-target row-count reconciliation per table after ETL
Preconditions: ETL completed for patient, encounter, obs tables; reconciliation report available
Test_Steps: 1. Capture source row counts per table before load | 2. Run the ETL load | 3. Capture target row counts per table | 4. Compare counts accounting for documented filters
Test_Data: Source: patient=10000, encounter=85000, obs=500000
Expected_Result: Target counts equal source counts minus documented exclusions for each table; any variance is itemized with reason; zero unexplained delta

Title: Negative: silent row drop on transform error is detected by reconciliation
Preconditions: One transform rule throws on malformed rows that are skipped without logging
Test_Steps: 1. Seed source with 5 rows that trigger the silent transform exception | 2. Run ETL | 3. Run row-count reconciliation | 4. Compare expected vs actual loaded counts
Test_Data: Source=1000 rows; 5 malformed; expected loaded=1000 (none should be silently dropped)
Expected_Result: Reconciliation detects a 5-row shortfall and fails the run; the 5 rows are surfaced in a rejects table rather than silently lost

Title: Field-level checksum reconciliation for critical clinical columns
Preconditions: Reconciliation computes per-column hash/aggregate on source and target
Test_Steps: 1. Compute aggregate checksum of obs.value_numeric on source | 2. Run ETL | 3. Compute the same aggregate on target | 4. Compare checksums and aggregate sums
Test_Data: Source SUM(value_numeric)=1234567.89
Expected_Result: Target aggregate equals source aggregate exactly; any mismatch identifies affected rows for investigation

Title: Sampled field-by-field comparison of migrated patient demographics
Preconditions: Random sample of N migrated patients with source-of-truth available
Test_Steps: 1. Draw a random sample of 200 migrated patient records | 2. For each, compare name, DOB, gender, identifiers against source | 3. Record mismatches | 4. Compute field accuracy rate
Test_Data: Sample size=200; acceptance>=99.5% field accuracy
Expected_Result: Field accuracy meets or exceeds 99.5%; every mismatch is logged with source and target values for remediation

Title: Date format transformation normalizes mixed source formats to ISO 8601
Preconditions: Transform maps DD/MM/YYYY and MM-DD-YYYY sources to ISO 8601
Test_Steps: 1. Load rows with dates in DD/MM/YYYY and MM-DD-YYYY | 2. Apply date normalization transform | 3. Inspect stored canonical dates
Expected_Result: Both normalize to 2005-04-03 in ISO 8601; ambiguous-format rows without a declared source pattern are rejected rather than guessed

Title: Code-system mapping transforms legacy diagnosis codes to ICD-10
Preconditions: Mapping table ICD-9 to ICD-10 loaded; unmapped codes routed to exceptions
Test_Steps: 1. Load encounters with ICD-9 diagnosis codes | 2. Apply the code-system mapping transform | 3. Verify mapped target codes | 4. Inspect unmapped exceptions
Test_Data: ICD-9 250.00 -> ICD-10 E11.9; ICD-9 V99.9 unmapped
Expected_Result: Mapped codes are correctly translated to ICD-10; unmapped code V99.9 is routed to the exceptions queue, not dropped or defaulted

Title: Decision table for null-handling strategy by target field nullability
Preconditions: Transform null-policy: NOT NULL target with no default -> reject; nullable -> pass null; coded with default -> apply default
Test_Steps: 1. For each target field type apply a null source value | 2. Evaluate the transform null-handling decision | 3. Record outcome per field class
Test_Data: Fields: birthdate(NOT NULL,no default); middle_name(nullable); status(coded,default=active)
Expected_Result: NOT NULL no-default field rejects null row; nullable field stores null; coded-with-default field applies "active"; outcomes match the table

Title: Concept mapping resolves source observation codes to OpenMRS concepts
Preconditions: OpenMRS concept dictionary loaded; source-to-concept map configured
Test_Steps: 1. Load observations keyed by source-specific codes | 2. Apply concept-mapping transform to OpenMRS concept_id | 3. Verify obs link to correct concept | 4. Inspect unmapped codes
Test_Data: Source code WT -> concept Weight (kg); source code BP-S -> Systolic BP
Expected_Result: Each source code resolves to the correct OpenMRS concept_id with proper datatype; unmapped source codes are quarantined for dictionary extension

Title: Foreign-key integrity verified across patient-encounter-obs hierarchy post-load
Preconditions: Target DB constraints active; load completed
Test_Steps: 1. Query for obs rows whose encounter_id has no matching encounter | 2. Query for encounter rows whose patient_id has no matching patient | 3. Query for orphaned order rows | 4. Compile orphan report
Test_Data: Expected orphans=0 across all FK relationships
Expected_Result: No orphaned obs, encounters or orders exist; all FK relationships resolve; report shows zero referential violations

Title: Negative: load order violation creates dangling reference and is caught
Preconditions: Loader configured with dependency ordering; test injects out-of-order load
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to load encounters before their parent patients | 2. Observe loader behavior | 3. Inspect integrity check result
Test_Data: Encounter batch references patient IDs not yet loaded
Expected_Result: Loader either defers/queues the dependent rows or fails fast; integrity check reports the dangling references; no committed orphan rows remain

Title: Identifier remapping preserves cross-table references after key reassignment
Preconditions: Migration reassigns surrogate keys; ID crosswalk table maintained
Test_Steps: 1. Load patient with source key 5001 reassigned to target key 90001 | 2. Verify all child encounters and obs repoint to 90001 via crosswalk | 3. Spot-check a referential chain end to end
Test_Data: Source patient_id=5001 -> target=90001; 3 encounters, 12 obs
Expected_Result: All child records correctly repoint to the new key 90001; crosswalk is complete and reversible; no record retains the stale source key

Title: UTF-8 multibyte names survive migration without mojibake
Preconditions: Source and target declared UTF-8; names contain accented and non-Latin characters
Test_Steps: 1. Load records with names containing accents, CJK and diacritics | 2. Run ETL | 3. Retrieve names from target and compare byte-for-byte
Test_Data: Names: Jose Muller (with diacritics), Zhang Wei (CJK), Bjork
Expected_Result: All multibyte characters round-trip exactly; no mojibake, no replacement characters; stored and displayed names match source

Title: Negative: Latin-1 source mislabeled as UTF-8 is detected, not corrupted
Preconditions: Encoding-detection/validation step precedes load
Test_Steps: 1. Provide a Latin-1 encoded file declared as UTF-8 containing high-byte characters | 2. Run the encoding validation step | 3. Inspect outcome
Test_Data: Latin-1 bytes for accented e declared as UTF-8
Expected_Result: Encoding mismatch is detected and the file is rejected or transcoded with an explicit conversion log; no silent corruption is written to target

Title: Boundary: 4-byte emoji and supplementary-plane characters in free-text notes
Preconditions: Target column is utf8mb4 capable
Test_Steps: 1. Load a clinical note containing a 4-byte supplementary-plane character | 2. Run ETL | 3. Retrieve and compare the note
Test_Data: Note text including a 4-byte supplementary-plane glyph
Expected_Result: The 4-byte character is preserved intact (utf8mb4); no truncation at the multibyte boundary and no insertion error

Title: CSV delimiter and quote collision in free-text fields handled correctly
Preconditions: Source export uses RFC-4180 quoting for fields containing delimiters
Test_Steps: 1. Load a record whose address field contains commas, double quotes and embedded newline | 2. Parse and load | 3. Retrieve and compare field
Test_Data: Address="12, Main St "Apt 4" \n Floor 2"
Expected_Result: Field is parsed as a single value with internal commas, escaped quotes and newline preserved; no column shift or row split occurs

Title: Failed migration run rolls back atomically leaving target unchanged
Preconditions: Migration wrapped in a transaction or has a defined rollback procedure; snapshot/baseline captured
Test_Steps: 1. Capture target baseline state | 2. Start migration run | 3. Inject a fatal error midway (e.g., constraint violation) | 4. Observe rollback | 5. Compare target to baseline
Test_Data: Baseline target rows=10000; failure injected after 4000 rows staged
Expected_Result: All 4000 partial rows are rolled back; target returns exactly to the 10000-row baseline; no partial/committed data remains

Title: State transition of a migration batch: pending to running to committed or rolled-back
Preconditions: Batch status model: PENDING, RUNNING, VALIDATING, COMMITTED, ROLLED_BACK, FAILED
Test_Steps: 1. Create a batch (PENDING) | 2. Start it (RUNNING) | 3. Pass validation (VALIDATING) | 4a. Commit (COMMITTED) | 4b. Alternatively force validation failure to drive ROLLED_BACK | 5. Attempt an illegal transition from COMMITTED back to RUNNING
Test_Data: Batch B1 happy path; Batch B2 validation-fail path
Expected_Result: Legal transitions succeed and are audited; illegal transition COMMITTED->RUNNING is rejected; terminal states are immutable

Title: Rollback restores prior values for updated (not just inserted) records
Preconditions: Migration performs in-place updates with before-image captured
Test_Steps: 1. Record before-image of 50 records targeted for update | 2. Run update migration | 3. Force failure after partial update | 4. Trigger rollback | 5. Compare records to before-image
Test_Data: 50 records, failure after 30 updated
Expected_Result: All updated records are restored to their before-image values; no record retains a half-applied update

Title: Idempotent re-run after rollback does not create duplicates
Preconditions: Migration designed to be idempotent via natural-key upsert
Test_Steps: 1. Run migration, force failure, roll back | 2. Re-run the same migration to completion | 3. Reconcile row counts and check for duplicate natural keys
Test_Data: Same source batch run twice (once failed+rolled back, once full)
Expected_Result: Re-run completes cleanly with no duplicate natural keys; final counts match a single clean run; idempotency holds

Title: Post-migration smoke validation confirms key clinical entities are retrievable
Preconditions: Migration committed to target; application layer points at migrated DB
Test_Steps: 1. Open a sample migrated patient in the application | 2. Verify demographics, active problems, recent encounters, latest vitals render | 3. Repeat for 10 representative patients
Test_Data: 10 representative patients spanning specialties
Expected_Result: All sampled patients render with intact demographics, problems, encounters and vitals; no missing sections or load errors

Title: Aggregate clinical metrics match between legacy and migrated systems
Preconditions: Both legacy and migrated systems queryable for the same reporting period
Test_Steps: 1. Compute active-patient count, encounter count and order count per month in legacy | 2. Compute the same in migrated system | 3. Compare period by period
Test_Data: Reporting period: last 24 months
Expected_Result: Aggregate metrics match within documented tolerance for every period; discrepancies are itemized and explained

Title: Latest-observation correctness: most recent vital per patient is preserved
Preconditions: Source-of-truth latest vitals known for a validation cohort
Test_Steps: 1. For a 100-patient cohort, identify the latest source weight/BP | 2. Query the migrated latest weight/BP | 3. Compare value, unit, datetime and provider
Test_Data: Cohort=100 patients; compare latest weight and BP
Expected_Result: Migrated latest observation matches source value, unit, datetime and provider for every patient; ordering by datetime is preserved

Title: FHIR Patient resource validity for migrated records
Preconditions: Migrated records exposed via FHIR R4 Patient endpoint; validator available
Test_Steps: 1. Retrieve migrated patients via GET /fhir/Patient | 2. Validate each resource against the R4 Patient profile | 3. Check identifier system URIs, gender value set and birthDate format
Test_Data: Sample of 50 migrated FHIR Patient resources
Expected_Result: All resources are structurally valid R4 Patients; identifier.system URIs are populated, gender is from the value set and birthDate is ISO 8601

Title: FHIR Observation references resolve to migrated Patient and Encounter
Preconditions: Migrated Observations exposed via FHIR R4 with subject and encounter references
Test_Steps: 1. Retrieve migrated Observations via GET /fhir/Observation | 2. For each, resolve subject and encounter references | 3. Confirm referenced resources exist and codes use proper system URIs
Test_Data: Sample of 50 migrated FHIR Observations
Expected_Result: Every subject and encounter reference resolves to an existing resource; LOINC/UCUM system URIs are correct; no broken references

Title: HL7 v2 ADT feed loads historical admissions with correct PID mapping
Preconditions: HL7 v2 ADT^A01 inbound interface configured to a parsing/loader pipeline
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a batch of ADT^A01 messages with PID, PV1 segments | 2. Process through the loader | 3. Verify patient identity (PID-3), name (PID-5), admission (PV1) in target
Test_Data: ADT^A01 with PID-3=MRN12345, PID-5=Tester^Pat, PV1 admit datetime
Expected_Result: Each ADT message creates/updates the correct patient and visit; PID-3 maps to identifier, PID-5 to name, PV1 to visit; malformed segments are rejected with NAK

Title: Negative: HL7 message with missing required PID-3 is rejected with NAK
Preconditions: HL7 inbound validates required fields before persistence
Test_Steps: 1. Submit an ADT message lacking PID-3 (patient identifier) | 2. Process through the interface | 3. Inspect ACK/NAK and target state
Test_Data: ADT^A01 with empty PID-3
Expected_Result: Interface returns an application reject (AR/AE) NAK; no partial patient is created; the rejected message is logged for reprocessing

Title: Data profiling report surfaces cardinality, null-rate and pattern anomalies
Preconditions: Profiling tool can scan source extract and emit column statistics
Test_Steps: 1. Run the profiler over the source extract | 2. Review per-column null rate, distinct count and dominant value patterns | 3. Flag anomalies such as 100% null or single-value columns | 4. Note candidate quality issues
Test_Data: Source extract: ~30 columns across patient and encounter
Expected_Result: Profiling report lists null rates, cardinality and value-pattern distributions; anomalies (all-null, all-same, unexpected formats) are highlighted for follow-up

Title: Primary natural-key uniqueness enforced during staging load
Preconditions: Staging table has unique constraint on natural key (e.g., national identifier)
Test_Steps: 1. Load a batch containing two rows with the same national identifier but conflicting demographics | 2. Run staging load | 3. Inspect rejects and constraint behavior
Test_Data: Two rows NID=900112233 with different birthdates
Expected_Result: The duplicate-key conflict is detected; one row is rejected to a conflicts table with both versions retained; staging keeps a single authoritative row

Title: Identifier check-digit validation rejects malformed national identifiers
Preconditions: Identifier validation rule includes check-digit algorithm (e.g., Luhn/modulo)
Test_Steps: 1. Load records with valid and invalid identifier check digits | 2. Run identifier validity check | 3. Inspect pass/fail per row
Test_Data: Valid NID=79927398713; Invalid NID=79927398710
Expected_Result: Valid check-digit identifier passes; invalid one is flagged "failed check digit" and quarantined; reason captured

Title: PHI is masked in migration logs and rejects/exception tables
Preconditions: Logging policy requires PHI masking; rejects table stores hashed/tokenized identifiers
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger validation failures that route rows to the rejects table | 2. Inspect migration logs and the rejects table | 3. Search for clear-text identifiers, names and birthdates
Test_Data: Rejected rows containing NID, name, DOB
Expected_Result: Logs and rejects table contain masked/tokenized PHI only; no clear-text identifier, name or DOB is exposed; access is audit-logged

Title: Migration service account uses least-privilege and actions are audited
Preconditions: Dedicated migration service account with scoped privileges; audit logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as the migration service account | 2. Attempt an out-of-scope action (e.g., user administration) | 3. Run a normal migration step | 4. Review the audit trail
Test_Data: Migration account scoped to load/read on staging+target
Expected_Result: Out-of-scope action is denied; permitted migration steps succeed and are recorded in the audit trail with actor, timestamp and entity counts

Title: REST API exposes batch validation results with paging and filtering
Preconditions: Migration API endpoint returns validation results; auth token available
Test_Steps: 1. POST a batch for validation | 2. GET /migration/batches/{id}/results?status=invalid&page=1 | 3. Verify paging, filter and field-level reason codes in the response
Test_Data: Batch with 5000 rows, 120 invalid; page size=50
Expected_Result: API returns only invalid rows when filtered, correctly paged with total counts and per-row reason codes; unauthorized requests are rejected 401/403

Title: Truncation guard: source value longer than target column is rejected not silently cut
Preconditions: Target column has fixed max length; transform has length guard
Test_Steps: 1. Load a row whose name field exceeds the target column length | 2. Run the transform | 3. Inspect stored value and reject behavior
Test_Data: Source name length=300; target max=255
Expected_Result: The over-length value is rejected (or routed to remediation) rather than silently truncated to 255; reason captured; data integrity preserved

Title: Boundary: value exactly at target column max length is accepted
Preconditions: Target column max length 255 inclusive
Test_Steps: 1. Load a name of exactly 255 characters | 2. Run the transform | 3. Verify storage
Test_Data: Source name length=255; target max=255
Expected_Result: The 255-character value is accepted and stored intact; 256 characters would be rejected by the truncation guard

Title: Timestamp timezone normalization preserves instant across source zones
Preconditions: Transform normalizes source local times to UTC with recorded offset
Test_Steps: 1. Load encounter datetimes from sources in two different timezones | 2. Apply timezone normalization | 3. Compare stored UTC instants and displayed local times
Expected_Result: Both resolve to the same UTC instant 2025-03-10 14:00Z; original offset is retained for display; no DST off-by-one error

Title: Boundary: DST spring-forward gap timestamp handled deterministically
Preconditions: Transform defines policy for non-existent local times during DST gap
Test_Steps: 1. Load a local timestamp that falls in the DST spring-forward gap | 2. Apply normalization | 3. Inspect resolved instant against policy
Test_Data: Local time 2025-03-09 02:30 in a US zone (gap)
Expected_Result: The gap time is resolved per documented policy (shifted forward or rejected) consistently; no crash and no arbitrary value

Title: Delta/incremental load applies only changed rows since watermark
Preconditions: Incremental loader tracks a high-water mark on last-modified timestamp
Test_Steps: 1. Perform initial full load | 2. Modify 10 source rows and insert 5 new ones | 3. Run incremental load | 4. Verify only the 15 changed/new rows are applied
Test_Data: Watermark after full load; 10 updates + 5 inserts
Expected_Result: Only the 15 changed/new rows are processed; unchanged rows are untouched; watermark advances; counts reconcile to +5 net inserts and 10 updates

Title: Negative: late-arriving record older than watermark is not skipped
Preconditions: Incremental loader handles out-of-order/late records via change capture
Test_Steps: 1. After establishing a watermark, introduce a backdated source change with an older modified timestamp but new change-capture sequence | 2. Run incremental load | 3. Verify the late record is captured
Test_Data: Late record modified-ts before watermark but new CDC sequence
Expected_Result: The late-arriving change is captured and applied (not skipped due to timestamp watermark); reconciliation reflects it

Title: Pairwise matrix across source-system, encoding and date-format combinations
Preconditions: Adapter layer supports multiple source systems, encodings and date formats
Test_Steps: 1. Build a pairwise matrix of {source: OpenEMR, HAPI, omiiCARE} x {encoding: UTF-8, UTF-16, Latin-1} x {date: ISO, DD/MM, MM-DD} | 2. Run migration for each selected combination | 3. Validate output correctness
Test_Data: Pairwise-reduced set covering all pairs at least once
Expected_Result: Every combination migrates correctly with proper transcoding and date normalization; no combination produces corruption or wrong dates

Title: Lineage/audit trail links each migrated record to its source and transform version
Preconditions: Lineage metadata captured: source key, batch id, ruleset version, load timestamp
Test_Steps: 1. Migrate a batch | 2. Select a target record | 3. Inspect its lineage metadata | 4. Trace back to the originating source key and transform ruleset version
Test_Data: Target record from batch B7 ruleset v2.3
Expected_Result: Each migrated record carries source key, batch id, transform ruleset version and load timestamp; lineage is queryable end to end for audit

Title: Migration cutover sign-off gate blocks go-live on unresolved critical defects
Preconditions: Cutover checklist with reconciliation, validity and sign-off gates; defect tracker integrated
Test_Steps: 1. Run all reconciliation and validation reports | 2. Leave one critical reconciliation defect open | 3. Attempt to transition the migration to GO-LIVE | 4. Resolve the defect and retry
Test_Data: One open Critical reconciliation mismatch
Expected_Result: Go-live transition is blocked while the Critical defect is open; after resolution and re-validation the gate permits cutover; decision is audited with approver identity
Healthcare_Classification: Patient Safety; Data Integrity; Regulatory/Compliance; Audit
